warningDesc:
This plugin is not allowed to be used as a tool to monitor others' behavior.
Any data generated and used by this plugin, such as notifications/reports/logs/status records/screenshots/screen recordings/variable names, etc., are for personal convenience only and are not to be used as any legal basis/evidence.
The developer of this plugin does not collect any of your data, but the two SaaS providers used by this plugin may have their own privacy policies.
That is to say, your device name and device identifier will be transmitted as part of the URI in the form of plain text webhook or WS to the servers running Bark and GoEasy. Although they cannot be seen by any man-in-the-middle under TLS encryption, the SaaS providers can see them in principle.
Please make sure you have read and agreed to their privacy policies and terms of service.
barkApiBaseLinkDesc:
The baselink for the Bark API, please obtain it after downloading the Bark app.
Example: https://api.day.app/AbCdEfGhIjKmLnOpQrStUv
displayNoticeWhenBarkNotiSentDesc: When enabled, a notification will pop up on this machine after reporting the online behavior to the phone.
onlineLocalmachineNoticeText: This online behavior has been reported to the phone
barkOnlineNoticeContentHeader: SiYuan has just started on this device:
onlyEnableListedDevicesDesc: When the switch is on, the plugin functionality is enabled only on the devices listed in the table. When the switch is off, the plugin functionality is enabled on all devices.
enableDeviceList: List of Devices to Enable Online Notifications
enableDeviceListDesc: This restriction only applies to Bark online notifications, as not all devices need online notifications. The devices listed in the table will have the plugin functionality enabled when the switch is on. Please avoid manual editing of this table.
addCurrentDeviceIntoListDesc: Add the current device to the table
removeCurrentDeviceFromListDesc: Remove the current device from the table
hintDesc:
• ● Made by zxkmm, open-sourced under the GPL license.
  • ● If you like this plugin, please give my GitHub repository a free star⭐ (Star).
  • ● Link: siyuan_online_devices_mgr
goeasySwitchDesc: This feature requires you to correctly fill in the GoEasy token.
goeasyTokenDesc: Please refer to the following link to obtain the token: Link
encryptionPasswordDesc: Mandatory. Messages sent through GoEasy are end-to-end encrypted with this password (AES-256, derived via PBKDF2), so GoEasy can only ever see ciphertext. You MUST use the exact same password on every device you want to control. The password is stored locally in SiYuan only and is never transmitted to GoEasy. If you have SiYuan sync enabled, this plugin setting (including this password) is automatically synced across your devices, so you usually only need to set it once. If left empty, the online device management feature will not run.
encryptionPasswordMissing: Please set the Encryption Password first (it must match across all your devices).
decryptFail: Failed to decrypt a received message (the encryption password does not match the sender's, or the data is corrupted). The message was ignored.
barkMsgSwitch: Send Device Online Notification via Bark
barkMsgSwitchDesc: This feature requires you to correctly fill in the GoEasy webhook baselink.
textSendBroadcastClipboardPlaceholder: This will be sent to all online devices. Note: if the target device doesn't have a clipboard manager, the previous clipboard content will be overwritten and cannot be retrieved.
textSendMessagePlaceholder: e.g., Don't look at my notes!
textSendToClipboardPlaceholder: This will send content to the target device's clipboard. Note: if the target device doesn't have a clipboard manager, the previous clipboard content will be overwritten and cannot be retrieved.
textSetAutoPasswordPlaceholder: Enter new Access Auth Code, please remember it carefully, otherwise you may have difficulty (or impossible) to unlock that device later.
textSetAutoPasswordConfirmPlaceholder: Please enter the Access Auth Code again to confirm
textPasswordMismatch: The two Access Auth Codes entered do not match, please try again.
textPasswordHistoryDesc: Records the access codes set from this device in case you forget them.
textLockFail: Lock failed, most likely because the remote device has no access auth code set. Please use the 'Set Access Auth Code' feature first.
textManageSnippetsDesc: Maintain a table of named code snippets. You can add, edit and delete snippets here, then run any of them on a remote device from the device panel.
textManageSnippetsSettingHint: Code snippets are managed from the dock panel's 'Manage Code Snippets' button.
textSnippetCodePlaceholder:
JavaScript. `request(apiPath, data)` calls the SiYuan kernel API, e.g.:
return await request("/api/system/version", {});
textNoSnippets: No code snippets yet. Click 'Add Snippet' to create one.
textSnippetRunning: Running snippet on the remote device...
textSnippetTimeout: Timed out waiting for the remote device to run the snippet (it may be offline or the snippet took too long).
textConfirmDeleteSnippet: Are you sure you want to delete this snippet?
goeasyTokenMissing: Please fill in the GoEasy Token first
barkUrlMissing: Please fill in the Bark API Baselink first
name: Online Devices Manager
beggingDesc:
This plugin is open source and free, so I need your encouragement.
If you like the features this plugin brings you, you can consider donating to help me with basic living expenses 🍚: Donate. (Donations won't unlock additional features)
If you're not financially comfortable, you can encourage me by giving a free ⭐ star to my GitHub repository.
If you or someone you know needs my skills, feel free to hire me anytime.
beggingInfoText:
(This message appears only with a very small probability, and will automatically disappear and never show again once I have collected enough stars)

disclaimerBody:
This plugin (siyuan_online_devices_mgr, “Online Devices Manager”) is free and open-source software released under the GNU General Public License (GPL). Please read every item below before enabling it. By checking the box below you confirm that you have read, understood, and accept all of the following terms.
  • 1. Arbitrary Remote Code Execution. The “Remote Code Snippets” feature runs user-supplied JavaScript on the remote device with access to request(...) (the full SiYuan kernel API) and, on the SiYuan Desktop (Electron) frontend, window.require — that is full Node.js access including child_process. This is functionally equivalent to SSH / shell access on the remote machine: it can read, write or delete all notes and files, launch any application, exfiltrate data and execute arbitrary system commands. Do not enable this feature on, and do not share your encryption password with, any device you do not fully own and trust.
  • 2. The Encryption Password is the Sole Key. End-to-end encryption (AES-256-CBC, PBKDF2) protects message confidentiality in transit against GoEasy, but anyone who knows your encryption password can connect as you and send commands — including code snippets — to all of your devices. Choose a strong, unique password, treat it like an SSH private key, never reuse it and never transmit it over insecure channels.
  • 3. No Authentication of Senders. The plugin has no user accounts and no signatures. Anyone holding the shared encryption password + GoEasy token can impersonate any device. It is designed for a single trusted owner managing their own devices; it is not a multi-user or shared-device tool.
  • 4. SaaS Provider Metadata Exposure. Although message content is E2E-encrypted, device presence data (device name, UUID, online/offline state) traverses GoEasy unencrypted by design (its presence API). Device names/IDs are also sent as part of URIs to Bark for notifications. Under TLS these are hidden from network observers, but GoEasy and Bark can see them in principle — read their privacy policies before use.
  • 5. GPL Open Source & No Warranty. This plugin and SiYuan itself are both licensed under the GNU General Public License (GPL). Under the GPL (§15/§16), the software is provided “AS IS”, without any warranty of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, or non-infringement, and without liability for damages arising from its use. Consistent with the GPL, the author makes no express or implied warranty regarding reliability, availability, accuracy, security, or fitness for any particular purpose, and does not guarantee the software will be error-free or operate without interruption.
  • 6. Free Software & Donations. The plugin is free to download and use; no functionality is paywalled or sold. Donations to the author are purely voluntary personal gifts to a natural person and are NOT a purchase of the software, a service contract, technical-support entitlement, or any upgrade of warranty. A donation does not create any commercial or service relationship between you and the author and does not increase the author’s liability in any way. The software remains free and provided without warranty regardless of whether you donate.
  • 7. Scope of Liability Limitation. Given that this is a free, GPL-licensed, personal-use, non-commercial tool that you have voluntarily chosen to use after being informed of the risks above, to the maximum extent permitted by the laws of the People’s Republic of China, neither the author nor the SiYuan project shall be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising out of or relating to the plugin (including, without limitation, data loss, device lockout due to a forgotten access auth code, loss of profits, or business interruption). This limitation applies only to losses not caused by the author’s intent or gross negligence.
  • 8. Statutory Floor Not Excluded. Nothing in this disclaimer excludes or limits any liability that cannot be excluded or limited under the mandatory provisions of the laws of the People’s Republic of China. In particular, pursuant to Article 506 of the Civil Code of the PRC, clauses exempting liability for personal injury to the other party, or for property loss caused to the other party by intent or gross negligence, are void and are hereby NOT relied upon; nor does this disclaimer affect any non-excludable statutory rights you may have. If any provision herein is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect (severability).
  • 9. Author & SiYuan Not Responsible for Your Conduct and Third Parties. The author and the SiYuan project are not responsible for: (a) your own configuration choices and use of the plugin (e.g. setting a weak encryption password, the devices you choose to enable, the code snippets you choose to write and run); (b) any act of a third party who obtains your encryption password or GoEasy token; (c) the acts, privacy practices, or security of the SaaS providers (GoEasy, Bark) or any other third-party service; (d) your failure to comply with applicable laws. You use the plugin entirely at your own risk. All data generated by the plugin (notifications, logs, command results, etc.) is for personal convenience only and has no legal, evidentiary, or contractual validity.
  • 10. Consent State is User-Editable Open Data. Because SiYuan and this plugin store all data — including the “accepted / declined” flag for this disclaimer — as open, plaintext, freely editable files and settings, you are technically able to change the consent flag to “declined” or delete it at any time by editing the storage; the plugin imposes no lock on this. Changing the flag will cause this disclaimer to be shown again on the next launch, and the relevant features will be disabled until you re-accept.
  • 11. Technical Inference of Prior Consent. Conversely, from a technical standpoint, if the online-device-management feature has ever taken effect on a given device (for example: GoEasy connected, the dock panel appeared, or a snippet ran), that necessarily means at some earlier moment on that same device this disclaimer was displayed and was affirmatively accepted — the plugin’s control flow does not allow these features to initialize otherwise. This is a factual statement about the plugin’s code logic, not a legal presumption about any specific individual.
  • 12. Intended Use, Prohibited Misuse & Compliance Responsibility. This plugin is strictly for managing your own personally-owned devices. Using it to monitor, control, surveil, or access another person’s device without their informed consent is prohibited and may violate the laws of your jurisdiction (e.g. computer-misuse, wiretapping, and personal-information-protection laws). You are solely responsible for complying with all applicable laws (data-protection/GDPR-style regulations, computer-crime statutes, employment-monitoring laws, and cryptography export controls) in your jurisdiction. The author only provides the tool; you bear all legal responsibility for how you use it.
  • 13. Software Defects, Supply Chain & Open-Source Acceptance. The existence of defects (bugs) is a normal phenomenon in software development; no complex software can be guaranteed to be absolutely defect-free. This plugin is open-sourced under the GPL; the full source code (including its direct dependencies, build scripts, and third-party libraries) is publicly available for audit and inspection. By downloading, installing, or using this plugin, you are deemed to acknowledge the foregoing and to accept the content and quality of the code. The author does not warrant that the plugin itself is free of defects, nor that its third-party dependencies or software supply chain are free of defects, backdoors, or malicious code (including, without limitation, risks introduced via hosting/distribution channels such as npm or GitHub). To the maximum extent permitted by the laws of the People’s Republic of China, the author shall not be liable for any loss or damage (including, without limitation, data loss, data leakage, system compromise, or business interruption) directly or indirectly caused by any such defect, backdoor, malicious code, or supply-chain attack; you bear the associated risk yourself. Before using this plugin in any critical environment, you are advised to audit the source code and dependencies yourself, pin dependency versions, and review the security advisories and known vulnerabilities of each dependency.
If you do not accept any of the above, do not enable the plugin. By checking the box below you confirm that you have read, understood, and accept all of the above terms.
disclaimerAcceptLabel: I have read and fully understand, and I accept all of the above terms
disclaimerNotAcceptedDesc: The online device management feature stays disabled until you accept the disclaimer. You can re-open it at any time via the "View Safety & Legal Disclaimer" button in the dock panel or the settings panel.
textNicknamePlaceholder: Enter nickname for this device, or leave blank to remove nickname.
blockWhenEditSwitchDesc: When enabled, other online devices will see a blur overlay that blocks editing while this device is actively writing. Signals are only sent when more than one device is online, to avoid unnecessary API usage.
textRemoteEditing: Another device is currently editing — please wait
